description:
## Bug

`parse_bp` (parser.rs:1245–1262) unconditionally appends a trailing `do … end`
block as the last arg of the preceding call. `parse_if` calls `parse_expr` for
the condition, so:

```
if pred(h) do
  [h | filter(pred, t)]
else
  filter(pred, t)
end
```

parses `pred(h) do [h|…] end` as `pred(h, do_block)`, leaving the `else` token
unexpected. Single-line `if n == 0 do … else … end` works only because `n == 0`
is a binop, not a call.

## Fix

Thread a `no_trailing_do_block` flag through `parse_bp` (e.g. via a wrapper
`parse_cond_expr` or a new arg). Use it in:
- `parse_if`'s cond
- `parse_while`'s cond (search parser.rs for callers)
- `case … do`'s scrutinee
- the guard expression after `when`

The trailing-do-block sugar continues to apply in **statement** position
(macro-call-with-block, item-level `test("…") do … end`) — unchanged.

## Tests

Add parser unit tests in `do_block_sugar_tests` (or sibling) covering:
- `if pred(h) do BODY else BODY end` parses BODY as the if-arm
- `if pred(h), do: e1, else: e2` keyword form still works
- `case f(x) do … end` parses subject as `f(x)`, not `f(x, do_block)`
- macro-call-with-block (`test("…") do … end`) still parses unchanged

## Independent of other children. Leaf.
title: fz-aiz.1: parser cond-context — suppress trailing-do sugar in cond position
